context: =  inflammation of bone and marrow caused by bacteria (most commonly pyogenic bacteria + mycobacteria), fungi, parasites, viruses Predisposed:   immunosuppression, diabetes mellitus, sickle cell disease, intravenous drug abuse, alcoholism Source of infection: (a)  direct inoculation: open fracture / direct trauma (commonly in young adults) Location:   tibia, wrist, femur, rib, thoracolumbar spine Dx:   requires 2 out of 4 of the following criteria Acute Pyogenic Osteomyelitis Age:     most commonly affects children Organism: (a) newborns: S. aureus, group B streptococcus, E. coli (b) children:
